children.heading: Contracts, grants-in-aid, and loans to groups, individuals, public agencies, and private nonprofit organizations; availability of appropriations; guidelines
children.label: a Contracts, grants-in-aid, and loans to groups, individuals, public agencies, and private nonprofit organizations; availability of appropriations; guidelines
i
children.text: For the purpose of carrying out , there are authorized to be appropriated to the National Endowment for the Arts $125,800,000 for fiscal year 1991 and such sums as may be necessary for fiscal years 1992 and 1993.section 954(c) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: 1991 and 1992 not less than 25 percent of the amount appropriated for the respective fiscal year; and

children.label: II
children.text: 1993 not less than 27.5 percent of the amount appropriated for such fiscal year;

children.label: I
children.text: 1991 and 1992 not less than 5 percent of the amount appropriated for the respective fiscal year; and

children.label: II
children.text: 1993 not less than 7.5 percent of the amount appropriated for such fiscal year;

children.label: B
children.text: For the purpose of carrying out , there are authorized to be appropriated to the National Endowment for the Humanities $119,900,000 for fiscal year 1991 and such sums as may be necessary for fiscal years 1992 and 1993. Of the sums so appropriated for any fiscal year, not less than 20 per centum shall be for carrying out .section 956(c) of this titlesection 956(f) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: Sums appropriated pursuant to subparagraph (A) and subparagraph (B) for any fiscal year shall remain available for obligation and expenditure until expended.

The Chairperson of the National Endowment for the Arts and the Chairperson of the National Endowment for the Humanities, as the case may be, shall issue guidelines to implement the provisions of paragraph (2) and paragraph (3). Such guidelines shall be consistent with the requirements of section 954(e), section 954()(2), section 956(f), and , as the case may be, regarding total Federal support of activities, programs, proj­ects, or productions carried out under authority of this subchapter.l1section 956(h)(2) of this titleSee References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Availability of appropriated unexpended funds; notice of availability of funds by advance appropriation
children.label: b Availability of appropriated unexpended funds; notice of availability of funds by advance appropriation

children.text: Sums appropriated pursuant to subsection (a) for any fiscal year shall remain available for obligation and expenditure until expended.

In order to afford adequate notice to interested persons of available assistance under this subchapter, appropriations authorized under subsection (a) are authorized to be included in the measure making appropriations for the fiscal year preceding the fiscal year for which such appropriations become available for obligation.

There are authorized to be appropriated to the National Endowment for the Arts $21,200,000” for fiscal year 1991 and such sums as may be necessary for fiscal years 1992 and 1993, to administer the provisions of this subchapter, or any other program for which the Chairperson of the National Endowment for the Arts is responsible, including not to exceed $50,000 for each such fiscal year for official reception and representation expenses. The total amount which may be obligated or expended for such expenses for fiscal year 1995 through the use of appropriated funds or any other source of funds shall not exceed $100,000.2So in original. The closing quotation marks probably should not appear.2

There are authorized to be appropriated to the National Endowment for the Humanities $17,950,000 for fiscal year 1991 and such sums as may be necessary for fiscal years 1992 and 1993, to administer the provisions of this subchapter, or any other program for which the Chairperson of the National Endowment for the Humanities is responsible, including not to exceed $50,000 for each such fiscal year for official reception and representation expenses. The total amount which may be obligated or expended for such expenses for fiscal year 1995 through the use of appropriated funds or any other source of funds shall not exceed $100,000.

children.label: A
children.text: $167,060,000 for fiscal year 1986,

children.label: B
children.text: $170,206,400 for fiscal year 1987, and

children.label: C
children.text: $177,014,656 for fiscal year 1988.

children.heading: Prohibition of grants to production workshops using admission proceeds for unauthorized purposes
children.label: e Prohibition of grants to production workshops using admission proceeds for unauthorized purposes
children.text: No grant shall be made to a workshop (other than a workshop conducted by a school, college, or university) for a production for which a direct or indirect admission charge is asked if the proceeds, after deducting reasonable costs, are used for purposes other than assisting the grantee to develop high standards of artistic excellence or encourage greater appreciation of the arts and humanities by our citizens.

children.heading: Availability of appropriations for arts education
children.label: f Availability of appropriations for arts education

children.text: Subject to subparagraph (2), in any fiscal year in which the aggregate amount appropriated to the National Endowment for the Arts exceeds $175,000,000, 50 percent of such excess shall be available to carry out .section 954a of this title
In each fiscal year, the amount made available to carry out shall not exceed $40,000,000, in the aggregate.section 954a of this title
Funds made available to carry out shall remain available until expended.section 954a of this title
